mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on future earnings and eases financial conditions, so equity prices rise over the next month. The signal is valid when the policy rate is in a downward trend and fails when cuts follow a recession shock already priced by equities.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment rises from an already elevated level and fails if higher unemployment is offset by aggressive policy easing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS accelerate together, payroll growth and retail spending confirm broad demand strength, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if payroll gains are not accompanied by spending growth, because employment alone without consumption does not sustain equity upside.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS/RSAFS expansion does not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of joint-expansion instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next month. The mechanism fails if industrial production improves only because of inventory restocking without end-demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals a housing-demand contraction that transmits to consumer wealth, construction activity, and cyclical earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 performance over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when housing starts are falling from a low base and fails if mortgage easing offsets the housing slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least half of instances or if the average return difference versus non-trigger periods is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS implies negative real policy rates, which support equity multiples by reducing real borrowing costs and encouraging risk-taking. The effect should appear over about one month and fails if inflation is rising faster than policy remains accommodative, causing discount-rate fears to dominate.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with CPIAUCSL accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains below 1% do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive value over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS turns down while UNRATE turns up, labor demand is deteriorating faster than households can absorb, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon signal fails if layoffs are immediately offset by policy support or if the labor data are revised away after the fact.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger does not produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if its hit rate is not better than either proxy used alone.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in GDPC1 accompanied by weak RSAFS indicates economy-wide demand contraction, lowering earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is regime-conditional because it is strongest during recessionary slowdowns and fails in rebound phases when markets price recovery before macro data turn.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction effect disappears outside recessionary windows.
